marketSignals.title: Task automation outpaces job elimination
marketSignals.detail: Anthropic's March 2026 labor-market analysis finds high occupational exposure to AI but no measurable unemployment impact yet. The gap between technical capability and actual organizational adoption remains substantial.

marketSignals.title: Clerical and admin roles face the steepest displacement pressure
marketSignals.detail: The ILO's May 2025 refined exposure index and BLS projections both confirm clerical support roles face the highest substitution risk, with observed employment declines in data entry, tellers, and cashiers.

marketSignals.title: Knowledge work is being reshaped, not replaced
marketSignals.detail: The OECD's June 2025 research distinguishes substitution from complementarity. Software developers, analysts, and managers see high task exposure but strong job growth — AI augments productivity rather than eliminating these roles.

marketSignals.title: Net job creation still outpaces displacement globally
marketSignals.detail: The WEF Future of Jobs 2025 projects 92 million jobs displaced but 170 million created globally by 2030, for a net gain of 78 million roles driven by AI-adjacent, green economy, and care economy growth.

marketSignals.title: Entry-level positions face disproportionate pressure
marketSignals.detail: Federal Reserve and Anthropic data show hiring slowdowns concentrated among workers aged 22-25 in exposed occupations, while senior and strategic roles remain resilient due to judgment, accountability, and stakeholder complexity.

methodology.summary: Employment data comes from the BLS national OEWS (May 2024 release). Displacement pressure scores combine task digitization, repeatability, current AI product velocity, physical execution requirements, human trust requirements, and regulatory friction. Critically, scores reflect displacement pressure on the role — not guaranteed job elimination. Research consistently shows that high task exposure often correlates with job transformation rather than replacement. Horizons through 2 years reflect near-term adoption pressure; 3-to-5-year views extend scenarios using WEF 2030 employer expectations and BLS 2024-2034 projections. Scores are calibrated against observed 2025-2026 labor market data from Anthropic, ILO, OECD, and the Federal Reserve.
